dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.safetyDescription: Only basic features are enabled. Advanced features require manual activation.

backup.title: Config Backups
backup.label: Label
backup.confirmRestore: Restore this backup? Current config will be backed up first.
backup.noBackups: No backups yet. Create your first backup to protect your config.

telegram.name: Telegram Group Assistant
telegram.description: Set up OpenClaw as a Telegram group chat AI assistant

discord.name: Discord Bot
discord.description: Configure OpenClaw as a Discord bot

llm-provider.name: LLM Provider
llm-provider.description: Quickly connect an LLM provider (DeepSeek, OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama)

vps-headless.name: VPS Headless Deploy
vps-headless.description: Deploy OpenClaw on a headless VPS server

wizard.prereqNotInstalled: OpenClaw is not installed. Please install it first.
wizard.completeDesc: Your configuration has been saved successfully.

telegram.step1Desc: Open Telegram, search for @BotFather, and send /newbot. Follow the prompts to create a new bot. Copy the token it gives you.
telegram.step2Desc: Paste the bot token you received from @BotFather. It looks like 123456789:ABCdef...
telegram.step3Desc: Select which AI model provider your Telegram bot should use for replies.

discord.step1Desc: Go to the Discord Developer Portal, create a new Application, then add a Bot to it. Copy the bot token.
discord.step2Desc: Paste the bot token from your Discord application settings.

llmProvider.step1Desc: Select the AI model provider you want to connect.
llmProvider.step2Desc: Sign up on the provider's website and copy your API key.
llmProvider.ollamaNote: Ollama runs locally. Make sure it's installed and running on your machine.

vpsHeadless.step1Desc: Daemon mode runs OpenClaw as a background service that starts automatically.
vpsHeadless.step2Desc: Set a secret token to secure remote access to your OpenClaw gateway.

wizard.apiKeyHint: Stored locally only. Never uploaded anywhere.

modelGuide.title: Choose your AI Model Provider
modelGuide.recommendation: Not sure which one? We recommend DeepSeek — it's free to start and works great.

deepseek.name: DeepSeek
deepseek.features: Great for Chinese, simple signup (~2 min)

openai.name: OpenAI
openai.features: Most popular globally, requires payment (~5 min)

ollama.name: Ollama
ollama.features: Completely free! Requires a good GPU

steps.keyLocalOnly: Your key is stored locally only and never uploaded anywhere.

settings.title: Settings
settings.uninstallDescription: Stop daemon, remove system service, and uninstall OpenClaw. Your config can be backed up first.

conservative.name: Conservative
conservative.description: Only chat and config management. Recommended for beginners.

standard.name: Standard
standard.description: Adds browser control, file access, and skills. For users with some technical background.

full.name: Full Access
full.description: All features enabled. For developers and advanced users.

welcome.title: Welcome to ClawSquire
welcome.subtitle: Your OpenClaw companion app
welcome.openclawNotFound: OpenClaw is not installed on this system.
welcome.installGuide: Install OpenClaw first, then come back here.
welcome.safetyNotice: ClawSquire starts with the safest settings by default. You can always enable more features later.

gateway.title: What is a Gateway?
gateway.explanation: The Gateway is OpenClaw's "brain" — it runs on your computer, receives your messages, calls AI models to process them, and sends back results.
gateway.tip: Most users should choose "Run on this computer".

node.title: What is a Node?
node.explanation: A Node is any device (phone, computer) that can act as OpenClaw's "hands and feet" — taking photos, reading contacts, getting location, etc.
node.tip: Your phone can be a Node via the official iOS/Android app.

agent.title: What is an Agent?
agent.explanation: The Agent is the AI assistant itself. It can help you with tasks like answering questions, browsing the web, or writing code.
agent.tip: The agent's abilities depend on which permissions you grant.

llmProvider.title: What is an LLM Provider?
llmProvider.explanation: An LLM Provider is the company that provides the AI "brain" (like DeepSeek, OpenAI, Anthropic). You need to register with one to get an API Key.
llmProvider.tip: Think of it like a restaurant membership — you need a card to order food.

apiKey.title: What is an API Key?
apiKey.explanation: An API Key is a secret password that lets OpenClaw use an AI service. Each provider gives you one when you register.
apiKey.tip: Keep it private! Never share your API Key with anyone. ClawSquire stores it locally only.

daemon.title: What is a Daemon?
daemon.explanation: A Daemon is a background service that keeps OpenClaw running all the time, even when you're not actively using it.
daemon.tip: Like a night watchman — always on duty, even while you sleep.

sandbox.title: What is Sandbox Mode?
sandbox.explanation: Sandbox mode limits what the AI can do on your computer. It prevents it from accessing files outside a safe area or running dangerous commands.
sandbox.tip: We strongly recommend keeping Sandbox ON, especially when you're new.

channel.title: What is a Channel?
channel.explanation: A Channel is the way you communicate with your AI assistant — like Telegram, WhatsApp, or Discord.
channel.tip: You can set up multiple channels, but one is enough to get started.

skill.title: What is a Skill?
skill.explanation: A Skill gives the AI new abilities — like browsing the web, writing code, or managing files. Skills can be installed and removed.
skill.tip: Start without extra skills. You can always add them later.

workspace.title: What is a Workspace?
workspace.explanation: The Workspace is the folder where the AI mainly operates. It reads and writes files here. Think of it as the AI's "desk".
workspace.tip: Choose a dedicated folder for this. Don't use your home directory.

config.title: What is a Config?
config.explanation: The Config (configuration) is a file that stores all your settings — which AI provider to use, which channels, permissions, etc.
config.tip: ClawSquire manages this file for you. You don't need to edit it manually.

token.title: What is a Token/Auth?
token.explanation: A Token is a security credential that prevents unauthorized people from controlling your AI. It's like a lock on your front door.
token.tip: Always set a gateway token. ClawSquire enables this by default.

gatewayWontStart.question: Gateway won't start?
gatewayWontStart.answer: Try these steps: 1) Check if OpenClaw is properly installed (run Health Check). 2) Check if another process is using port 18789. 3) Restart the daemon from Dashboard.

whereToGetApiKey.question: Where do I get an API Key?
whereToGetApiKey.answer: Go to Setup → LLM Provider. We'll guide you through creating an account and getting your API Key step by step.

whatIsSandbox.question: What is Sandbox mode? Should I enable it?
whatIsSandbox.answer: Sandbox mode limits what the AI can do on your computer. We strongly recommend keeping it ON, especially as a beginner. It prevents the AI from accessing sensitive files or running dangerous commands.

howToCreateBot.question: How do I create a Telegram Bot?
howToCreateBot.answer: Go to Setup → Telegram Group Assistant. The wizard will guide you through creating a bot with @BotFather and connecting it to OpenClaw.

doctorCheckFailed.question: Health Check failed — what do I do?
doctorCheckFailed.answer: Click on the failed check to see details. Most issues have a 'Fix' button that will resolve them automatically. If not, the help text explains what to do manually.

isMyDataSafe.question: Is my data safe?
isMyDataSafe.answer: Yes. ClawSquire runs entirely on your computer. Your API keys, config files, and all data stay local. Nothing is uploaded to any server. ClawSquire is open source (MIT license) — you can verify this yourself.

canIUndo.question: Can I undo changes?
canIUndo.answer: Yes! Use Backups to save snapshots of your configuration. You can compare changes and restore any previous version at any time.

whatIfSomethingBreaks.question: What if something breaks?
whatIfSomethingBreaks.answer: Don't worry! ClawSquire automatically creates a backup before making changes. You can always restore from Backups. If OpenClaw itself has issues, run Health Check for diagnosis.
